matter.title: Riverbend Analytics LLC v. Coastal Provisions LLC
matter.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC moves in Sagadahoc County Superior Court to confirm a commercial arbitration award entered in its favor arising out of a technology services agreement, and to reduce it to an enforceable judgment; Coastal Provisions LLC cross-moves to vacate the award, contending the panel refused to hear material evidence.

fact_pattern.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC moves in Sagadahoc County Superior Court to confirm a commercial arbitration award entered in its favor arising out of a technology services agreement, and to reduce it to an enforceable judgment; Coastal Provisions LLC cross-moves to vacate the award, contending the panel refused to hear material evidence.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Riverbend Analytics LLC and Coastal Provisions LLC were parties to a technology services agreement containing a mediation-then-arbitration clause. Their dispute was submitted to a single arbitrator under AAA Commercial Rules, which on 2026-01-18 issued a reasoned award in favor of the petitioner in the amount of approximately $5539594.

Riverbend Analytics LLC now applies to the Superior Court under Maine's Uniform Arbitration Act, 14 M.R.S. ch. 706, to confirm the award and enter judgment on it. Coastal Provisions LLC resists confirmation and cross-applies to vacate the award, asserting that the panel refused to hear material evidence. Because judicial review of an arbitration award is narrow, the court will not reweigh the merits and may set the award aside only on the exclusive statutory grounds in 14 M.R.S. § 5938.

This is a record-based, motion-practice matter rather than a trial: there is no jury and essentially no discovery. The parties brief the cross-applications on the arbitration record, and the court decides whether to confirm under 14 M.R.S. § 5937, modify or correct under § 5939, or vacate under § 5938. The petitioner also seeks fees, costs, and post-award interest under 14 M.R.S. § 5943.
undisputed_facts: The parties agreed to arbitrate disputes arising out of a technology services agreement pursuant to a mediation-then-arbitration clause.
The dispute was heard by a single arbitrator under AAA Commercial Rules and an award issued on 2026-01-18.
The award was entered in favor of the petitioner in an amount of approximately $5539594.
disputed_facts: Whether any of the exclusive statutory grounds to vacate the award under 14 M.R.S. § 5938 are established, including whether the panel refused to hear material evidence.
Whether the arbitrators exceeded the authority conferred by the parties' arbitration agreement.
Whether the award should be confirmed as entered, modified or corrected, or vacated.
Whether the petitioner is entitled to fees, costs, and post-award interest under 14 M.R.S. § 5943.

timeline.event: Application to confirm the award filed in Bath Superior Court under 14 M.R.S. § 5937.
a single arbitrator under AAA Commercial Rules issues the arbitration award in favor of the petitioner.

third_parties.name: Lighthouse Partners Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Prior affiliation said to bear on the alleged failure to disclose a conflict.

third_parties.name: Marcus K. Poe
third_parties.relevance: Testified at the arbitration hearing on the merits of the underlying dispute.

third_parties.name: Summit Logistics Co.
third_parties.relevance: The opposing party in the underlying contract dispute that produced the award now before the court.

exchanges.question: What did the arbitration decide, and how much did you win?
exchanges.answer: We arbitrated our dispute over a technology services agreement and the arbitrator ruled for us, awarding about $5539594. We want that turned into a court judgment we can actually enforce.

exchanges.question: What is the other side doing about it?
exchanges.answer: Instead of paying, they are trying to vacate the award. Their theory is the panel refused to hear material evidence. We think it is just a do-over attempt because they lost.

exchanges.question: How hard is it for them to undo an arbitration award?
exchanges.answer: My understanding is the court does not retry the case and can only vacate on a few narrow statutory grounds. We want you to hold them to that deferential standard.

exchanges.question: What is the urgency for you?
exchanges.answer: Time and money. Every month this drags on is a month we cannot collect. We want the award confirmed and reduced to judgment as quickly as possible, with interest running.

exchanges.question: Are you looking to recover anything beyond the award itself?
exchanges.answer: Yes. We want our fees and costs for the confirmation fight and the post-award interest the statute allows. We should not have to eat the cost of their losing motion.

exchanges.question: Would you consider resolving this short of a ruling?
exchanges.answer: Only if they pay essentially the full award promptly. We are not interested in a discount that rewards them for filing a meritless vacatur motion.

goals: Confirm the arbitration award under 14 M.R.S. § 5937 and reduce it to an enforceable judgment as quickly as possible.
Defeat the respondent's cross-motion to vacate under the deferential statutory standard of 14 M.R.S. § 5938.
Recover attorney fees, costs, and post-award interest under 14 M.R.S. § 5943.
priorities: Speed: converting the award into a collectible judgment without unnecessary delay.
Holding the court to the narrow scope of review so the merits are not re-litigated.
Preserving and maximizing the monetary recovery, including interest.
desired_outcomes: An order confirming the award in full and entering judgment for the award amount plus interest.
Denial of the cross-motion to vacate and an award of fees and costs to the petitioner.
non_starters: Any vacatur or remand that reopens the arbitrated merits.
A negotiated discount that effectively rewards the respondent's meritless vacatur motion.

constraints.budget: Cost-conscious but committed to fully defending the award and pursuing fees and interest.
constraints.timeline: Wants the award confirmed and reduced to judgment promptly so collection and post-award interest are not delayed.

issues.issue: Whether the petitioner is entitled to fees, costs, and post-award interest
sub_questions: Does the statute or the award support an award of fees and costs on confirmation?
From what date does post-award interest run on the judgment?
issues.client_position: The petitioner is entitled to costs and post-award interest, and to fees to the extent the award and statute allow.

issues.issue: Whether the arbitration award should be confirmed and judgment entered on it
sub_questions: Has a timely application to confirm been made?
Are there grounds to vacate, modify, or correct that would preclude confirmation?
issues.client_position: The award is valid and must be confirmed and reduced to judgment under 14 M.R.S. § 5937 and § 5941.

issues.issue: Whether the arbitrators exceeded their powers by awarding relief outside the contract
sub_questions: Did the award draw its essence from the parties' agreement?
Did the arbitrators decide only matters submitted to them?
issues.client_position: The arbitrators acted within the broad authority the parties conferred, so the 'exceeded powers' ground fails.

expert_opinions.topic: Arbitrator disclosure obligations and the alleged evident partiality
expert_opinions.opinion: The arbitrator's nondisclosure departed from accepted disclosure standards and supports a partiality challenge. (Offered to support the cross-motion to vacate; the court still reviews the award only under the narrow statutory grounds.)
expert_opinions.basis: Review of the AAA appointment record, the arbitrator's disclosures, and the parties' conflict correspondence.

evidence.title: Proof of the amounts awarded
evidence.description: Invoices, ledgers, and computations supporting the award amount and post-award interest.

evidence.title: Arbitration award and the arbitrator's reasoned decision
evidence.description: The signed award and the arbitrator's written reasons that the petitioner seeks to confirm.

evidence.title: Arbitrator's disclosures and AAA appointment record
evidence.description: The arbitrator's disclosure statements and the AAA record of appointment, central to the partiality challenge.

evidence.title: Arbitration hearing transcript and exhibits
evidence.description: The record of the merits hearing, offered on the claim that material evidence was refused.

amounts.label: Arbitration award amount
amounts.basis: The principal sum awarded by the arbitrator and sought to be confirmed.

amounts.label: Bond amount related to the underlying contract
amounts.basis: Surety bond on the underlying project potentially reached by the confirmed award.

amounts.label: Post-award interest
amounts.basis: Interest accruing on the award under 14 M.R.S. § 5943.

amounts.label: Attorney fees and costs sought on confirmation
amounts.basis: Fees and costs incurred in the confirmation proceeding.

amounts.label: Disputed punitive / consequential component
amounts.basis: The portion of the award the respondent contends exceeded the arbitrators' powers.

causes_of_action.claim: Application to confirm the arbitration award and for entry of judgment
elements: A valid arbitration agreement and a final award
No basis to vacate, modify, or correct the award
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order confirming the award and entering judgment for the award amount plus post-award interest

causes_of_action.claim: Application, in the alternative, to modify or correct the award
elements: An evident miscalculation, a description error, or an imperfection in form
A correction that does not affect the merits of the controversy
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Correction or modification of the award and confirmation as modified

causes_of_action.claim: Respondent's cross-application to vacate the award
elements: An exclusive statutory ground such as corruption, fraud, or undue means
Evident partiality or corruption of an arbitrator
The arbitrators exceeding their powers, or a refusal to hear material evidence
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order vacating the award and denying confirmation

affirmative_defenses.basis: No valid agreement to arbitrate this dispute existed, so there was no arbitral authority. 14 M.R.S. § 5928.
An arbitrator failed to disclose a conflict, showing evident partiality. 14 M.R.S. § 5938(1)(B).

motions.title: Motion to seal the arbitration record
motions.relief: An order sealing confidential portions of the arbitration record filed with the court.

motions.title: Motion to confirm the arbitration award
motions.relief: An order confirming the award under 14 M.R.S. § 5937.

motions.title: Cross-motion to vacate the arbitration award
motions.relief: An order vacating the award under 14 M.R.S. § 5938.

docket.entry: Arbitration award issued and served on the parties.
Application and motion to confirm the award filed.
Cross-motion to vacate the award filed.
Hearing / oral argument on the cross-motions held.

facts.arbitration_forum: a single arbitrator under AAA Commercial Rules
facts.vacatur_ground: the panel refused to hear material evidence
